sections.content: I can see from the images that the green frame with white center has moved closer to the green frame with black center. Let me check the exact position now and continue moving right. I need to understand the movement pattern - it seems to move 3 cells per RIGHT press. Let me check current position and plan the remaining moves.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current player position
seg = current_frame.segmentation
green_frame_hash = 'df604ed27e7e83b0'
player = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['hash'] == green_frame_hash]
print("Player green frames:")
for p in player:
    print(f"  id={p['id']}, boundary={p['boundary']}, children={p['children']}")

# Check what's in the children
for p in player:
    for cid in p['children']:
        child = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['id'] == cid][0]
        print(f"    child: color={child['color']}, boundary={child['boundary']}")

# The black-centered frame position
black_frame = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N' and len([c for c in n['children'] if seg['nodes'][[x['id'] for x in seg['nodes']].index(c)]['color'] == 'B']) > 0]
# Simpler: find the green frame whose child is black
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        for cid in n['children']:
            child = [x for x in seg['nodes'] if x['id'] == cid][0]
            if child['color'] == 'B':
                print(f"\nBlack-centered green frame: boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
